question: Install Git on the system and verify that git --version runs successfully.
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
if git --version > /dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; else exit 1; fi

question: Set your global Git user name to "Lab User".
Set your global Git user email to "lab.user@example.com".
Configure the default branch for new repositories to be "main".
Create a directory named "testrepo", initialize a Git repository inside it, and verify that the current branch is "main".
hint: Run: mkdir testrepo && cd testrepo && git init
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
mkdir -p /tmp/testrepo && cd /tmp/testrepo && git init > /dev/null 2>&1 && [ '$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)' = 'main' ] && exit 0 || exit 1
